title: Business Intelligence Analyst
description: **Description** The Finance Data Office (FiDO) FSGC (Finance \& Strategy Governance Committee) team is seeking a Business Intelligence Analyst. In this unique role, the analyst will serve as the FSGC Data SME for the Finance \& Strategy (F\&S) COFO, Marketing and M\&A teams, owning and auditing data mapping files, providing expert support, and ensuring data integrity and scalability. They will manage business change intake, translate requirements into metadata and mapping solutions, and support execution of validated updates across Foundation Data Model (FDM), Budget accountability mapping (Leaderview), Targets setting mapping for Corporate (Targets file), and data platforms like Workday, Hyperion Essbase and Snowflake. This role will collaborate with stakeholders across our Finance\+ stakeholders to elicit and analyze business requirements, evaluate existing processes, and identify gaps or inefficiencies. The analyst will translate business needs into clear, actionable documentation and will manage resource allocation to leverage various FiDO data and process capabilities, ensuring solutions are both scalable and effective. The role includes analyzing datasets, building reports and dashboards, collaborating with stakeholders and governing bodies, supporting large\-scale transformation projects, and maintaining documentation and training to enable efficient, aligned, and compliant data operations within FiDO to drive our vision of IMPACT \= PEOPLE x PROCESS x TRUSTED DATA x TECHNOLOGY forward. **Impact** * Serve as the FSGC Data SME for the F\&S COFO, Marketing and M\&A teams, responsible for owning, maintaining, and auditing data mapping files to meet business needs. Will also provide expert support, consultation, and issue resolution to ensure data mapping integrity, efficiency, and scalability. * Support the Finance \& Strategy Governance Committee (FSGC) by managing intake of business changes, translating requirements into effective metadata and mapping solutions, and executing approved updates with full validation across the Foundation Data Model (FDM), Budget accountability mapping (Leaderview), Targets setting mapping for Corporate and Sales (Targets file), and Hyperion cubes. * Analyze large datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and actionable insights, while cleaning and transforming data into accurate formats; generate reports, dashboards, and visualizations using Excel, Hyperion Essbase, Tableau, Snowflake, and other financial tools. * Gather and analyze business requirements from FiDO Finance\+ stakeholders, collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure alignment, and support testing and validation to confirm solutions meet business needs. * Collaborate with the FDMO (Foundation Data Model Office) and other governance bodies across Salesforce to align FSGC governance policies with the FDM governance model, while overseeing UAT to validate accurate implementation of FSGC changes in Workday and Hyperion. * Contribute to the design and ongoing development of Hyperion\-based cubes and Leaderview logic, partnering with Digital Enterprise Technology (DET) to deliver updated mappings across key reporting systems. * Contribute to the evaluation of an EPM tool to support F\&S planning and forecasting needs, with the potential to enable the retirement of Hyperion. * Using Tableau Prep, publish Leaderview mapping to critical data sources within Snowflake and Tableau Cloud. * Leverage and maintain co\-developed tools (e.g., Metamorpher, data logic sequencing, data consolidator, Tableau Prep flows) to streamline data integrity and availability. * Facilitate creation and maintenance of data contracts with Non\-Finance stakeholders to ensure alignment with FSGC requirements. * Co\-build monthly refresh cycle calendars, secure stakeholder alignment, and deliver F\&S Tableau Starter Workbook training and support, as needed. * Create and maintain process documentation, learning materials, and technical guides to ensure operational efficiency and knowledge sharing. * Play a key role in large\-scale transformation projects (e.g., EPM tool transition, FDM consolidation/rationalization, centralizing mapping within the CFO organization). * Create and support communication strategies and Slack posts to raise awareness of FSGC processes and dependencies. **Minimum Qualifications** * 2\-3\+ years work experience in FP\&A, Accounting, Consulting, Operations, or Data preferably at a public company with experience in data analytics, curation and validation * Experience with multi\-dimensional databases, e.g. Hyperion, Workday or other financial systems * Ability to manage multiple projects and processes in a fast paced, dynamic environment with competing priorities * Medium to advanced skills in Excel * Stellar problem\-solving skills * Highly motivated, proactive, self\-aware, collaborative, flexible, responsive, positive and fun **Preferred Qualifications** * Self\-starter and high degree of motivation to go above and beyond the task at hand * Excellent communication and presentation skills. Ability to communicate effectively with different levels of management, business stakeholders, and technical communities * Experience with reporting and analytics applications is a plus, e.g. Snowflake, Tableau, etc. * Interest/Experience using AI tools would be a plus

title: Business Intelligence Developer
description: **Senior Data \& BI Engineer – (Sql Server/ SSIS or SSRS/ Cloud)** Our Dublin City Based client is seeking an experienced Data \& Business Intelligence Engineer with a background in designing large\-scale Data systems. Ideally someone with exposure to Sql Server and SSRS and Cloud data is ideal. **Job Responsibilities:** * Senior member of the development team of technical solutions, software, and applications, along with automated testing, documentation, and related processes to meet stakeholder requirements. * Guide the technical design efforts for the technical solution, engaging in architecture discussions at a portfolio level and collaborating with enterprise architecture initiatives. * Ensure alignment of technical solutions and applications with enterprise architecture standards, maintaining architectural coherence with the overall system architecture. * Develop code following established engineering standards. * Estimate development effort for the broader technical solution and applications within the team. * Ensure that assigned work meets quality, reliability, resilience, scalability, and stability goals. * Enhance the quality, reliability, resilience, scalability, and stability of all technical solutions and applications supported by the team. * Mentor and coach junior team members to assist in advancing their skills. * Operate and support applications and solutions developed by the team. * Actively participate in communication and processes adopted by the team. **Essential Experience:** * Understanding of Data Warehousing core concepts with practical implementation experience. * Strong expertise in SQL Server or PostgreSQL. * Python or C\# nice to have. * Solid background in data analysis, data understanding, pattern recognition, and behaviors. * Familiarity with data transformation tools and experience in ETL tools (e.g., SSIS, Alteryx, AWS Glue). * Competence in data visualization using tools like PowerBI, Tableau, and report development. * Familiarity with at least one cloud platform, such as Amazon AWS or Microsoft Azure. **Education Requirements** * 3rd Level degree in Data related course from a recognised institution. **Desirable Skills \& Experience:** * Experience with data virtualization, particularly Dremio. * Familiarity with Azure Functions (Blob Storage). **Benefits:** * Salary : €70\-85k plus bonus and strong benefits package * Mostly on site but some working from home per week as well * One of Ireland best Employers to work for. * Excellent Career growth and work lifer balance * Very Low Turnover within the firm. **HOW TO APPLY:** If you are interested in this role – please apply for this role with your updated CV

title: Buyer
description: **Buyer** **About Choice Stores** Choice Stores is a proudly 100% Irish\-owned retailer, known for great value, bright and welcoming stores, and teams who take pride in delivering a great customer experience. With a growing presence across Ireland, we're deeply committed to supporting local jobs, local suppliers, and the communities we serve. Our locally led business model keeps us agile, people\-focused, and responsive to what really matters: our teams and our customers. As we grow, we're looking for passionate, driven individuals to join us and be part of our journey. At the heart of that success is our Support Office\-where innovation meets execution. We're now seeking a dynamic and commercially aware Buyer to join our Buying team and help shape the product ranges our customers love. **Your Role:** As a Buyer at Choice Stores, you'll play a key role in selecting and sourcing product ranges that drive performance across our store network. You'll collaborate with suppliers, planners, and store teams to ensure that every range under your remit is commercially viable, operationally efficient, and aligned with customer demand. Skills \& Competencies * Strong analytical and problem‑solving mindset, able to troubleshoot complex technical challenges. * Organised, detail‑oriented, and focused on process excellence and quality delivery. * Effective communicator with stakeholder management experience. * Experience in scripting for automation. **Why you will love working here** * Work\-life balance: Hybrid working model for applicable roles, Full range of leave entitlements, Career Breaks and Secondments * Physical Wellbeing: Discounted gym membership, Eyesite tests, Flu Vaccinations, Paid sick benefit, wellbeing initiatives * Financial Wellbeing: Competitive salary, Defined contribution pension, Income protection 1:1 financial advice. * Learning \& Development: We promote a culture where you can continuously develop on a personal and professional basis. title: Cloud Infrastructure Engineer
description: **Description** Every day, Global Payments makes it possible for millions of people to move money between buyers and sellers using our payments solutions for credit, debit, prepaid and merchant services. Our worldwide team helps over 3 million companies, more than 1,300 financial institutions and over 600 million cardholders grow with confidence and achieve amazing results. We are driven by our passion for success and we are proud to deliver best\-in\-class payment technology and software solutions. Join our dynamic team and make your mark on the payments technology landscape of tomorrow. **Cloud Infrastructure Engineer** **Summary Of This Role** We are seeking a highly skilled Infrastructure Engineer to join our Technology Operations group in Dublin. This is a hands\-on engineering role with strong elements of operational engagement and technical ownership. The successful candidate will provide expertise across Google Cloud Platform (GCP), VM Infrastructure, Terraform, Kubernetes, and configuration management tools (Ansible or equivalent), while helping to define and drive Infrastructure standards, resilience, and reliability. The role spans build, run and operations of our cloud \& vm environment, supporting the full lifecycle from infrastructure\-as\-code through to day\-to\-day operations, on\-call, and continuous improvement initiatives. **What Part Will You Play?** * Implements and operates GCP infrastructure using Terraform (IaC) and Kubernetes (GKE / container orchestration). * Owns infrastructure configuration management (Ansible or equivalent). * Ensures security, scalability, and high availability across infrastructure platforms. * Supports cloud cost optimisation and capacity planning. * Participates in the on\-call rotation, providing tier\-3/4 support for critical infrastructure services. * Participates in incident response and root\-cause analysis for infrastructure and cloud issues. * Drives automation of monitoring, alerting, and remediation workflows. * Partners with cross functional teams to align delivery models (Kanban for BAU, Agile for projects). * Jira governance for Infrastructure projects — workflow / agile methodology * Produces technical assessments / documentation upholding standards and providing recommendations for management. * Deploys and maintains Kubernetes clusters (GKE). * Configures namespaces, RBAC, ingresses controllers, and services mesh as required. * Builds and deploys containerised workloads; manages scaling, upgrades, and patching. * Troubleshoots pod, node, and networking issues in production clusters. * Writes and maintains Ansible playbooks and roles for VM and cluster configuration. * Automates patching, security hardening, and system updates. * Standardises environment builds and enforce consistency across dev/test/prod. **What Are We Looking For in This Role?** **Minimum Qualifications** * Bachelor's Degree * 3\+ years’ experience as an Infrastructure Engineer, SRE, or Cloud Engineer in enterprise environments. * Proven expertise in Google Cloud Platform (GCP). * Strong skills in VM workloads, Terraform (IaC) and Kubernetes (GKE or equivalent). * Hands\-on with Ansible (or equivalent configuration management). * Broad knowledge of networking, databases, Linux systems, and hybrid infrastructure. * Experience with monitoring, observability, and incident response tooling. * English (at least C1\) **Preferred Qualifications** * GCP Professional Cloud Architect or equivalent certification or Hands on Experience. * Experience in regulated environments (PCI\-DSS, ISO, SOC2, etc.) an advantage * DevOps / SRE hands on experience **What Are Our Desired Skills and Capabilities?** * Strong communicator — able to influence both technical engineers and senior stakeholders. * Organised and pragmatic, with strong ownership and accountability. * Collaborative style — balances governance with delivery speed. * Demonstrated success implementing Kanban and Agile delivery within Infra/Cloud Ops. * Ability to document and enforce technical standards. * Proven track record of improving operational performance and cross\-team delivery.

title: Commissioning Engineer
description: **Location:** Field\-Based \- Remote with Travel **Employment Status:** Full\-Time \| Direct Hire **Compensation:** Competitive \+ Bonus **Travel:** Up to 75% \- Ireland, UK, Netherlands, Denmark and Finland **Who We Are** Thermo Systems is a global, full\-service control systems integration partner delivering premier class automation solutions across the **Energy, Life Sciences, and Mission Critical** markets. Guided by ***The Thermo Way***, we are unified, dynamic, and committed to delivering excellence. Our culture is grounded in three core principles: * **Employee Success** – investing in our people and their growth * **Customer Success** – delivering reliable, high\-quality solutions * **Financial Strength** – operating with discipline and long\-term focus We believe strong teams, trusted partnerships, and technical excellence drive sustainable success. **The Opportunity** The Commissioning Engineer will lead and execute advanced commissioning activities across mission\-critical projects, with a focus on BMS, EPMS, PLC, and SCADA systems. This role goes beyond field validation, requiring the ability to diagnose system issues, modify and develop control logic, and support system integration during commissioning and delivery phases. Working closely with Engineering, Integration, and Operations teams, you will play a key role in ensuring systems are not only installed correctly, but functionally robust, fully integrated, and aligned with design intent and operational requirements. This position is ideal for an experienced commissioning professional with strong controls expertise, capable of bridging the gap between design, software, and field execution in high\-availability environments. This position is ideal for professionals who value accountability, technical rigor, and hands\-on execution in environments where safety, performance, and reliability matter every day. **What You’ll Do** **Core Responsibilities:** * Lead commissioning activities for BMS, EPMS, PLC, and SCADA systems across mission\-critical/data centre projects * Perform advanced system validation including point\-to\-point checks, functional testing, and integrated systems testing (IST) * Diagnose, troubleshoot, and resolve complex system issues across controls, networking, and field devices * Modify, develop, and optimise PLC logic and SCADA/BMS software during commissioning phases * Support system integration between mechanical, electrical, and control systems to ensure cohesive operation * Validate control sequences under normal, failure, and redundancy scenarios (including failover testing) * Support and lead Level 3–5 commissioning activities, including client witnessing and sign\-off * Review design documentation, control narratives, and software to ensure alignment with field implementation * Produce and maintain detailed commissioning reports, test scripts, and issue logs **Collaboration \& Communication:** * Coordinate with cross\-functional teams to align scope, schedule, and deliverables. * Communicate clearly and proactively with internal teams, customers, and partners. * Identify and escalate risks, constraints, or scope changes early. **Quality, Safety \& Process** * Adhere to company policies, QMS requirements, and established safety standards. * Deliver accurate, high\-quality results with strong attention to detail. * Identify opportunities for process improvement and contribute to stronger execution and efficiency. **What We’re Looking For** **Education \& Experience:** * Degree or equivalent experience in Electrical Engineering, Controls, Automation, or related discipline * 5\+ years’ experience in commissioning, controls engineering, or system integration * Strong experience in mission\-critical/data centre environments **Skills \& Qualifications:** * Strong knowledge of BMS, EPMS, PLC, and SCADA systems (e.g., Rockwell, Siemens, Ignition, Schneider) * Experience developing or modifying PLC code and SCADA/BMS graphics during live project environments * Understanding of electrical systems, control architectures, and networked systems * Ability to interpret and validate control sequences, schematics, and design documentation * Proven troubleshooting capability across software, hardware, and integration layers * Experience title: Company Secretarial Trainee
description: **Job Description** **Company Secretarial Trainee** This is an excellent opportunity for an ambitious and driven candidate who is keen to progress within Ireland’s fastest growing professional services firm. We are looking to hire a Company Secretarial Trainee to join our team. We are looking for graduates/postgraduates who are ambitious and want to start their career in the area of company secretarial/governance. **Responsibilities** Reporting to the company secretarial management team, you will perform the usual duties associated with a trainee role: * Preparation and filing of statutory forms and attendance at the Companies Registration Office * Arranging for company formations and branch registrations * Updating the company secretarial database * Maintenance of company secretarial records to include updating Minute Books and Statutory Register * Dealing with queries and requests, on a day to day basis, from clients, auditors, solicitors and internal staff, including company searches * Taking ownership of a portfolio of clients and being responsible for ensuring that clients are meeting their statutory obligations * Keeping up to date with filings, and producing documentation in respect of the Annual General Meeting and other minutes, as required * Responsibility for billing clients and debtors’ collection * Filing, archiving and general administrative duties **Key Requirements** * Have a desire to complete corporate governance or company secretarial qualification * Experience in an office environment preferable however not essential * Excellent MS Office skills * Highly organised * Excellent attention to detail * An ability to prioritise and multi\-task is essential * Familiar with statutory obligations under the Irish Companies Act is an advantage * Computer literate with experience of word, excel and company secretarial package eg. GEMS, Blueprint etc is an advantage * Strong client focus with excellent oral and written communication skills **About Us** **We are Grant Thornton** Grant Thornton Ireland is rapidly approaching 3,000 people, in 9 offices across Ireland, Isle of Man, Gibraltar and Bermuda. With a presence in over 149 countries around the world and a global network of 73,000 people, we bring our clients the local knowledge, national expertise and global presence to help them succeed – wherever they’re located. At GT, we work as ***trusted advisors*** , bringing local knowledge and national expertise, with a global presence, to help businesses succeed – wherever they are located. We make business more personal by investing in building relationships and empowering our clients to make the right decisions for their organisation now and for the future. Whether that is working with the public sector to build thriving communities, with regulators and financial institutions to build trust, or with a diverse range of businesses to help them achieve their goals, Grant Thornton Ireland work hard to support clients to act on the issues that matter. **At GT Ireland we don’t just predict your future, we build it** . **A Career at GT** Looking for a more fulfilling role in professional services? One where fresh thinking, collaboration and diversity are valued? At Grant Thornton we do things differently. **What does this mean for you?** A career in a more inclusive working environment, a more collaborative work culture, a more supported, flexible working role, more possibilities to grow and more opportunities to help shape the future for your clients. We respect and value your experience. And we want you to bring your authentic self to work and be at your best. It is how it should be. **Grow with us** At Grant Thornton, we care about our people and work hard to make you feel valued. If you are looking to deepen and develop your skills, knowledge, and experience throughout your career, then that is what you will get, and more. **Our Benefits** Please follow this link for information on our generous benefits package. **About The Team** **Equity, diversity and inclusion** At Grant Thornton, we provide equitable opportunities for all our colleagues. We are a responsible, sustainable business where equity, diversity and inclusion (ED\&I) is at the forefront of our workplace culture agenda, and today, we continue to build and develop on our existing ED\&I structure and strategy to meet our workplace culture needs. People are at the heart of our business and teams built with varied individuals present diverse viewpoints, which need to be heard and valued. We are all at our best when we are able to be ourselves and we view integrity and authenticity as integral values to bring to our day\-to\-day work\-life at the firm. We are excited to see the personality and perspectives you will bring to our team because we know we will all benefit from them. Diversity of thought, background and experience enables better decision\-making, improves the quality of our delivery, and helps us to meet the needs of our clients. Our firm is built on people and their ideas, so we want to hear all the new perspectives and fresh thinking you have to offer. You form the bedrock of our firm’s best\-practice principles and we will champion you as leaders from day one. **Reward And Benefits** Our reward and benefits are designed to create an environment where our people can flourish. We are committed to building a culture where our people have access to the necessary benefits to help promote a healthy lifestyle and thrive. **Recognition** We want to create a culture of recognition and celebrating success, by saying thank you to people who surpass our expectations and recognising the right values and behaviours. Our Shout Out recognition scheme is our way of highlighting and promoting achievements. Whether you simply want to say thank you, celebrate a special occasion or give an award for doing something exceptional, you can do all of this and more through the scheme.

title: Compliance Analyst
description: A leading financial services organisation in Dublin is seeking a Compliance Analyst to join their dedicated Compliance Department. * Join a well\-established financial services organisation in Dublin with a strong reputation for nurturing talent and providing comprehensive training opportunities. * Play a key role in maintaining regulatory standards by conducting email reviews, supporting investigations, and enhancing compliance policies within a supportive team environment. **What you'll do:** * Conduct thorough reviews of email communications for trading groups, ensuring all correspondence adheres to regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Perform detailed searches related to internal investigations, collaborating closely with colleagues across departments to gather relevant information. * Prepare comprehensive weekly and monthly memos summarising findings from email surveillance reviews, presenting clear insights to senior management. * Assist in the development and enhancement of compliance policies and procedures, adapting them to reflect changes in business operations, regulations, and emerging issues. * Stay up\-to\-date with relevant regulatory developments and changes, proactively sharing knowledge with the wider team to ensure collective understanding. * Participate actively in other compliance\-related projects as needed, demonstrating flexibility and willingness to support organisational goals. * Work collaboratively with colleagues across departments to build effective working relationships that facilitate Securities Act 1933, Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA). * Excellent team player who thrives on co\-operation and collaboration within diverse groups. * Ability to prioritise tasks effectively in environments characterised by constant change and evolving demands. * Entrepreneurial mindset combined with proven ability to manage projects independently while remaining responsive to team needs. * Exceptional writing skills paired with analytical abilities that enable you to produce clear reports and solve complex problems. * Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint as well as other technological applications relevant to compliance work. * Broad knowledge of financial products and the wider financial services industry is considered advantageous for this role. * Strong interpersonal skills enabling you to build dependable relationships across departments. **Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates**

The success of any hotel comes, not with the decor and elaborate rooms, but from the friendliness and courtesy of the associates it employs. The success of Druids Glen Resort, we believe, depends on the commitment of each and every member of the team to practice teamwork, mutual respect, responsibility and professionalism. If you have previous experience and would be interested in the opportunity of career advancement within a 5 Star property contact us today. **Important Information:** All applicants must be eligible to live and work in the Ireland full time. You will be asked to provide documented evidence of eligibility.

title: Corporate Finance Associate
description: Barden have been exclusively engaged by a well known Irish company that is significantly growing and set to double in size within the next year. They are now building out their finance team in line with this growth and are creating a transactions sub\-team. The transactions team will focus on creating value and growth within the company. They are now inviting three corporate finance professional to join their team as **Corporate Finance Associate** . This is a unique opportunity at this level sitting on a close knit team, gaining direct exposure to a broad range of activities working closely with the senior leadership team. **ABOUT THE ROLE:** This role provides an excellent opportunity to utilise one’s corporate finance experience within an industry setting and add real value to a leading Irish business. Main duties and responsibilities will include: * Support the execution of acquisitions, disposals, and financing activities. * Assist with the development of financial models, investment analysis, and performance forecasting. * Contribute to due diligence efforts and manage data flow and documentation for live transactions. * Help create high\-impact presentations, internal papers, and investor materials. * Collaborate with internal stakeholders across multiple departments to gather insights and input for transaction work. * Liaise with external partners, including advisors, lenders, and investors, throughout deal lifecycles. * Monitor financial obligations and covenant compliance tied to active financing structures. * Identify areas for process improvement across processes. * Ad hoc projects as required. **ABOUT THE PERSON:** * ACA or CFA Qualified with 0\-3 years PQE; * Previous corporate finance, transactions services, tax, transfer pricing or investment experience a strong advantage; * Strong financial modelling skills is an advantage; * Advanced Excel skills; * Ability to deliver both qualitative and quantitative analysis for decision making at senior management level; * Good commercial acumen and ability to analyse qualitative information; * Good problem solving and analytical skills; * Excellent working knowledge of Excel. ***This is an ideal role for someone looking for the opportunity to advance their career in Corporate Finance and progress within a progressive and significantly growing company.*** *Barden’s Accounting and Tax recruitment practice is where finance professionals go before they start looking for a job. Make sure you get expert advice about your Accounting / Tax career from Barden first – your future is just too important to leave to chance.*

title: Credit Funds – Associate, Fund Controller
description: **Company Overview** KKR is a leading global investment firm that offers alternative asset management as well as capital markets and insurance solutions. KKR aims to generate attractive investment returns by following a patient and disciplined investment approach, employing world\-class people, and supporting growth in its portfolio companies and communities. KKR sponsors investment funds that invest in private equity, credit and real assets and has strategic partners that manage hedge funds. KKR’s insurance subsidiaries offer retirement, life and reinsurance products under the management of Global Atlantic Financial Group. References to KKR’s investments may include the activities of its sponsored funds and insurance subsidiaries. **Team Overview** KKR’s Global Finance team oversees the firm’s tax compliance, credit and accounting, and reporting matters to produce analytical insights that drive business decisions and long\-term success. The team is responsible for designing efficient frameworks through internal and external data and reports to understand the market environment and business dynamics and implement best practices. The team also collaborates closely with KKR’s Legal and Compliance team to ensure compliance and accurate reporting and performance of our corporate tax entities related to our investment funds. The Global Finance team also functions as a support for clients, from onboarding to investor communications and overall relationship management across all asset class, as well as responding to any ad\-hoc tax\-related requests. **Position Summary** KKR is seeking a qualified accounting professional to join its KKR Credit division in Dublin. The opportunity is to join the Private Credit Fund Controllership team, supporting a broad range of private credit and open\-ended fund structures across accounting, financial reporting, fund operations and strategic finance initiatives. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of success and advancement in prior roles and will be a person who has demonstrated strong technical fund and problem solving skills and is also someone who has a great appetite for knowledge and professional growth. **Key Responsibilities** * Perform fund accounting and operational activities across the private credit and evergreen platforms * Responsible for detailed partnership accounting and analysis, including complex allocations, management fee calculations, carried interest computations and investor\-level accounting * Prepare and review fund financial reporting, including financial statements, performance reporting, investor capital account statements and audit management * Oversee and review work performed by third\-party fund administrators, including NAV packages, reconciliations, investor reporting and control processes * Support fund operations including capital calls, capital distributions, financing activity and liquidity management * Respond to ad hoc investor requests and operational inquiries, with frequent interaction across marketing/client management, legal, tax and portfolio management teams * Coordinate and manage cross\-functional projects and strategic initiatives across finance and operations * Assist in driving accounting, reporting and business process enhancement and efficiency initiatives **Professional Experience / Qualifications** * Professional with 5\+ years of audit, fund accounting or asset management experience, including experience with private credit and/or open\-ended fund structures, partnership accounting and financial instruments * Experience overseeing third\-party fund administrators, including review of NAV packages, investor allocations, fee calculations and control processes * Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or a related discipline * Accounting qualification (ACCA/ACA/CIMA) * Strong verbal and written communications skills with the ability to interact with all levels of the organization * Advanced user of Excel and highly proficient with MS Word and MS PowerPoint * Prior experience with Investran and/or Geneva is preferred, but not required * Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively in a growing environment * Proven ability to manage multiple demands and projects in a fast\-paced work environment, including ability to prioritize time efficiently and handle heavy workloads while remaining attentive to detail * Highly developed planning and organizational skills; able to anticipate change, adjust responses accordingly, and initiate steps to maximize resources toward a common objective KKR is an equal opportunity employer. Individuals seeking employment are considered without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, sexual orientation, or any other category protected by applicable law. KKR will provide reasonable accommodations as required by applicable federal, state, and/or local laws. Individuals seeking an accommodation for the application or interview process should email Benefits@kkr.com. Emails sent for unrelated issues, such as following up on an application, will not receive a response. If you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you may request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access https://www.kkr.com/careers because of your disability. You can request reasonable accommodations by sending an email to Benefits@kkr.com. Only emails left for this purpose will be returned. **Massachusetts Applicants:** It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. This notice applies only to applicants and employees who work or will work in Massachusetts, in accordance with applicable state law.

title: Data Analyst
description: **Job Title** \- Data Analyst **Location** \- Dublin, Ireland **Mode** \- Hybrid **Type** \- Contract **POSITION GENERAL DUTIES AND TASKS :** * Lead data migration activities and analysis Work with Data scientists/Business Analysts/Product Owner to understand MI and dashboard requirements. * Design Data Solutions: Leverage your analytical skills to design innovative data solutions that address complex business requirements and drive decision\-making. * Create detailed field level mappings as needed for different MI Dashboards. * Develop and maintain comprehensive dashboards to visualize key performance indicators. * Conduct market research and trend analysis to inform strategic business decisions. * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure data accuracy and integrity. **Requirements** * Bachelor's degree in computer science, Information Technology, or a related field; advanced degree preferred. * Minimum of 8\+ years of experience in data engineering or a similar role. * Experience in the Pydata stack: Pandas, Numpy. * Knowledge of data modelling in Python and Excel * Intermedia Excel skills: countifs, sumifs, pivot table. * Strong proficiency in statistical analysis software and data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar. * Knowledge of relational database technologies such as Teradata, Oracle and Microsoft SQL with strong SQL execution and optimisation skills.

title: Data Governance Analyst
description: **Role Summary** **JOB DESCRIPTION** We are seeking a talented Data Governance Analyst. As a Data Governance Analyst within our business **,** you will be responsible for developing, maintaining, communicating, and promoting data management processes and controls. You assist with defining policies, notices, and standards, as well as promoting ongoing enhancements throughout the organisation. You will work closely with stakeholders in our Business, Compliance, Risk, and IT teams to enhance our retention and monitoring approach, ensuring best practice in data management. **Responsibilities** **RESPONSIBILITIES** **Data Governance Framework \& Standards** * Promote and embed data governance standards across Irish offices, business units and functions * Enhance and maintain the CFIL data governance framework, ensuring it is clearly communicated and understood by all staff * Develop, publish and maintain best practice guidance via internal communications, SharePoint, and formal guidance documents * Inform and advise stakeholders on data protection and privacy best practice. **Data Management \& Protection** * Identify unstructured data across the business and develop plans for appropriate management * Assist with Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs), including documentation, risk identification, and tracking of mitigating actions * Support the end‑to‑end coordination of Data Subject Access Requests (DSARs) and other Data Subject Requests (including rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection) in line with GDPR and internal procedures. * Maintain and update Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) in collaboration with business units * Support oversight of personal data processing activities across business functions and third‑party arrangements **Monitoring, Risk \& Compliance** * Prepare monthly management information that includes issue tracking, actions and any additional data protection information required for reporting * Develop and monitor systems and controls to detect and prevent poor data behaviours * Conduct audits to support the monitoring and enforcement of good data practices * Perform regulatory gap analysis against GDPR, DPC, DPO and CBI guidance, and develop remediation plans as required * Support investigation and appropriate reporting of data breaches * Clearly articulate data issue mitigation processes and escalation pathways. **Training \& Awareness** * Prepare and support the delivery of data protection training and awareness initiatives, including induction training and periodic refreshers. * Act as a first point of contact for routine data protection queries from staff, escalating complex matters as required. * Promote staff awareness and support managers with practical data protection guidance. **Business Critical Services Support** * Contribute to the delivery of CFIL outsourcing and operational resilience programmes as part of the Business Critical Services team. **Qualifications** **Requirements \& Skills:** * 2\-3 years’ experience in a data control, data governance, or data protection role, ideally in a financial services environment * Strong understanding of data protection principles and regulatory requirements; relevant qualification in Data Management or Data Protection desirable * Solid ICT knowledge, including how systems generate, store, and control data, and how technology can be leveraged to manage data risk * Good understanding of industry\-standard data governance frameworks and standards * Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret multiple data sets, conduct gap analyses, diagnose issues, and deliver practical, risk\-based solutions * Experience managing and influencing change to drive continuous improvement in a data\-focused environment * Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence business functions * Self\-motivated, organised, and capable of planning work around key project milestones.

title: Data Privacy Associate
description: **About Us** At Davy, it's the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team \- you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **Requirements** **About the role** An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Data Privacy Associate within the Data \&Analytics Team at Davy Group. This role is ideal for a motivated and adaptable self\-starter who thrives in a fast\-paced environment and can deliver work to tight deadlines. The successful candidate will be a strong team player who can also take ownership of individual tasks and contribute to the organisation's data governance and privacy objectives. **Role** This position plays a key role in supporting GDPR compliance and promoting data governance best practices across the organisation. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Manage and respond to queries related to personal data processing and handle Data Subject Rights requests within legal timeframes. * Coordinate and maintain the organisation's Record of Processing Activities (RoPA), including regular reviews, in collaboration with stakeholders such as Privacy Champions, Legal, and Third\-Party Management, to ensure accuracy and compliance. * Coordinate and support business units in completing Privacy Assessments (PAs), ensuring they are carried out effectively, risks are identified and monitored, and outcomes are documented in collaboration with stakeholders such as Privacy Champions, Legal, and Third\-Party Management. * Utilise privacy workflow tools to manage compliance activities effectively. * Assist with the implementation of data privacy policies, supporting procedures, and activities to ensure compliance with the GDPR and best practices. * Comply with the Risk and Compliance frameworks, policies and procedures associated with the role. * Collaborate with all areas of the organisation to implement practical GDPR solutions and drive data governance best practices. * Develop strong working relationships interdepartmentally to ensure cross functional collaboration across the organisation. * Participate in projects and deliver ad hoc tasks as required. **About You** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * Experience \& Background * 2\+ years financial services or legal industry experience. * Basic understanding of the GDPR and data privacy principles. * Technical Skills * A high degree of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, particularly Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. * Experience with privacy tools, such as Microsoft Purview and OneTrust is a distinct advantage but not essential. * Core Competencies * Highly organised, with the ability to manage multiple deliverables and meet strict deadlines. * Excellent attention to detail. * Ability to prioritise workload effectively. * Ability to handle confidential information with discretion. * Communication \& Collaboration * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to deliver complex information clearly and professionally. * Proactive and collaborative approach, with confidence to build relationships and engage stakeholders across the organisation. **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today's fast\-moving world, we know it's important to feel secure and empowered. That's why Davy benefits support you through all of life's stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values \- One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don't meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Design Engineer (Civil)
description: **Design Build Search** is seeking a **Design Engineer (Civil)** on behalf of our client based in Dublin 15\. This opportunity is suited to candidates who holds a minimum Level 8 Honours Degree in Civil or Structural Engineering, with approximately 3 to 6 years’ post\-graduate experience. **Responsibilities:** Working as part of a collaborative engineering team, and with guidance from senior staff, the successful candidate will: * Engage with clients, under the direction of the Project Manager, to deliver design solutions that meet project requirements. * Review and provide technical oversight of documentation and designs prepared by Graduate Engineers. * Prepare and check preliminary designs, technical reports, and detailed design drawings/specifications using a range of industry\-standard software packages. * Coordinate and manage the procurement process for Site Investigations and Building/Civil Engineering contracts. * Support Project Managers in the preparation of tender documentation and the evaluation of submitted tenders. * Assist in the administration of construction contracts, ensuring compliance with design intent, programme timelines, and budgetary constraints. * Report to and take direction from their Line Manager, Project Managers, and senior project team members as required. **Requirements:** * Strong written and verbal communication skills in English, along with effective interpersonal abilities. * Proficiency in AutoCAD 2D, with a willingness and aptitude to further develop skills in AutoCAD 3D and BIM platforms such as Autodesk Revit. * An interest in developing competence in additional software tools, including MicroDrainage, Microsoft Access, and GIS applications such as ArcGIS and MapInfo. * A working understanding of construction contract frameworks, including the Public Works Management Framework (PWMF) forms of contract. * Familiarity with relevant project and design software such as Microsoft Project, MicroDrainage, and other tools necessary to effectively perform daily responsibilities. This role presents an excellent opportunity for a Design Engineer seeking to further develop their career within a well\-established and reputable engineering consultancy.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: DevOps Engineer \*Stamp 4 or EU/UK passport required Contract: 12\-Month Daily Rate Contract Location: Galway, Ireland \| Hybrid Rate: €400 \- 440 A leading global financial services organisation is seeking a **DevOps Engineer** to join a high\-performing Developer Enablement team supporting cutting\-edge Equity Technology platforms used by Portfolio Managers, Traders, and Research teams globally. Your Responsibilities: * Design, build, and enhance **CI/CD pipelines** and **DevOps automation solutions** * Support cloud infrastructure initiatives with a strong focus on **AWS** environments * Develop and maintain performance testing frameworks and scripts using **JMeter** * Build dashboards and monitoring solutions using **DataDog** * Collaborate with engineering teams to standardise DevOps tools, practices, and security configurations About You * 3\+ years' experience in a Software Engineer or DevOps Engineer role * Strong hands\-on experience with **Jenkins, CI/CD pipelines** , and cloud infrastructure * Proven **AWS** experience, ideally within enterprise\-scale environments * Experience with performance testing tools such as **JMeter or Performance Center** * Background in software engineering with technologies such as **Java, C\#, Scala, or PL/SQL** This Senior Software DevOps Engineer contract offers the chance to join a highly collaborative and technology\-driven environment where innovation, autonomy, and continuous improvement are strongly encouraged.
